vBRIEFInfo.author: agent:plan
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1395: Ctrl+K palette — semantic conversation search with excerpts (Phase 2)

plan.title: Ctrl+K palette: semantic conversation search with excerpts (Phase 2)
plan.author: agent:plan

narratives.Problem: Phase 1 shipped a unified Ctrl+K palette (commit 77e0943c5) with commands / memory / observations / issues groups. It cannot search conversation CONTENT. Conversation embeddings today are session-level (one vector per conversation built from summary/tags via buildEmbeddingText) and cannot produce per-message excerpts. Users have no way to semantically find a past conversation by what was said in it and jump to the exact message.
narratives.Proposal: Add a chunk level to the existing conversation-embeddings system: a streaming JSONL chunker, a new Float32-BLOB SQLite chunk table that is lazily populated and idempotent on byteOffset, and brute-force cosine search over chunk vectors that returns excerpts using the existing memory-excerpt marker convention. Surface results as a Conversations group in the existing CommandPalette, selectable to deep-link /conv/:id scrolled to the matched message. Add a Conversation Search settings section with a provider/model/key picker, enable toggle, and a bulk-index cost guard. Reuse everything that already exists (providers.ts embed(), search.ts cosineSimilarity, discovered-sessions-db.ts vector helpers, config keys, the palette excerpt renderer); build only the chunk layer.
narratives.Constraints: No new embedding pipeline or provider — reuse embed(provider,{text,model,apiKey}). Indexing is LAZY / on first search (NOT an eager startup backfill); restarting the dashboard must not re-embed everything. Storage is SQLite Float32 BLOB + brute-force cosine — no native vector dep (no sqlite-vec). Excerpts use the existing marker convention, never injected HTML. Semantic search must be fully disable-able and must degrade gracefully when no provider/API key is configured.
narratives.Risks: Embedding cost on first/bulk index of many conversations (mitigated by the ~$1 cost-guard confirmation and lazy indexing). Brute-force cosine over many chunks could be slow for very large histories — acceptable for Phase 2 scope (current-project conversations), revisit if it regresses. Provider/key may be absent in headless/cron sessions — the disabled/empty state covers this.
narratives.Alternatives: Eager batch backfill / incremental watcher (rejected in favour of lazy-on-first-search per operator decision 2 — note this diverges from the issue body wording). sqlite-vec native vector index (rejected per decision 1 to avoid a native dependency). Lexical/full-text search (out of scope — this issue is semantic only).

autoDecisions.summary: Storage & vectors: SQLite Float32 BLOB + brute-force cosine (no sqlite-vec).
autoDecisions.rationale: Operator decision 1. A new per-message chunk table stores Float32 BLOB vectors and reuses the existing cosineSimilarity / topKCosine helpers, avoiding a native dependency.

autoDecisions.summary: Indexing trigger: lazy / on first search (not eager batch backfill).
autoDecisions.rationale: Operator decision 2. The first search of a conversation triggers chunk + embed + store; subsequent searches hit the cache. Idempotency on byteOffset makes appended JSONL cheap to re-index. This deliberately diverges from the issue body's 'incremental watcher / batch backfill' wording.

autoDecisions.summary: Embed provider: config-driven across openai / voyage / ollama, openai text-embedding-3-small default.
autoDecisions.rationale: Operator decision 3. Reuse the existing embed() provider abstraction; the search path must not assume OpenAI is always present and must offer a graceful disabled/empty state when no provider/key is configured.

autoDecisions.summary: Open-at-message: deep-link + scroll to the matched message via sequence/byteOffset.
autoDecisions.rationale: Operator decision 4. The parser already emits sequence and byteOffset; ConversationPanel / MessagesTimeline honor a scroll target so /conv/:id can land on the matched message.

narrative.Action: Add a chunker that streams a conversation JSONL session message-by-message and emits per-message chunks { sessionId, role, ts, byteOffset, charLength, text, sequence }, splitting long messages into ~512-token windows with overlap (short messages -> single chunk). REUSE the existing JSONL parsing — src/lib/conversations/jsonl-async.ts and parseConversationMessages() in src/dashboard/server/services/conversation-service.ts (already emit `sequence` and byte offsets). This item does NOT write to the DB — it only produces chunk records. byteOffset is the idempotency key consumed by the chunk-store item.

subItems.title: Streaming a session JSONL emits one or more chunks per message, each with sessionId/role/ts/byteOffset/charLength/text/sequence.
Long messages are split into ~512-token windows with overlap; short messages produce a single chunk.
byteOffset and sequence are stable and match parseConversationMessages() output, suitable as an idempotency key for re-indexing appended JSONL.
Unit tests cover: short single-message session, a long message that splits into multiple overlapping windows, and empty/whitespace edge messages.

items.title: Chunk-embeddings SQLite table (Float32 BLOB), lazy + idempotent

narrative.Action: Add a new SQLite table storing per-chunk embedding vectors as Float32 BLOBs, keyed on (sessionId, byteOffset), with columns for role/ts/charLength/text/sequence and the embedding provider/model/dim. Follow the existing patterns in src/lib/database/discovered-sessions-db.ts (loadEmbeddings, getEmbedding, topKCosine) and add the schema in src/lib/database/schema.ts. Provide upsert (idempotent on byteOffset) and load-by-session / load-all helpers. Table is LAZY-populated by the search item — no eager startup backfill here.

subItems.title: A new table is created via a schema.ts migration and stores Float32 BLOB vectors keyed on (sessionId, byteOffset).
Upsert is idempotent on byteOffset: re-indexing a conversation whose JSONL grew does not duplicate existing rows.
Load helpers return decoded Float32Array vectors compatible with the existing cosineSimilarity / topKCosine helpers.
Each row records embedding provider + model + dimension so stale-provider rows are detectable.
Unit tests cover upsert idempotency and BLOB encode/decode roundtrip.

items.title: Semantic chunk search + lazy indexing orchestration

narrative.Action: Add chunk-level semantic search. On the first search of a conversation, lazily chunk + embed + store its messages (idempotent on byteOffset); on subsequent searches reuse stored vectors. Embed the QUERY via the existing provider abstraction embed(provider, {text, model, apiKey}) in src/lib/conversations/embeddings/providers.ts (openai text-embedding-3-small default; voyage/ollama supported). Rank chunks by brute-force cosine (cosineSimilarity from src/lib/conversations/search.ts), group hits by conversation, and return each result with an excerpt around the best-matching message using the existing memory-excerpt marker convention (the U+2987/U+2988 marker pair, NOT raw HTML) so the frontend highlights safely. MUST degrade to an empty/disabled result (no throw) when no embedding provider/API key is configured OR semantic search is disabled in config.

subItems.title: First search of a conversation lazily indexes its chunks; subsequent searches reuse stored vectors with no re-embed.
The query is embedded via the existing embed() provider abstraction (config-driven provider/model/key).
Brute-force cosine ranks chunks; hits are grouped by conversation and the best chunk drives the excerpt.
Excerpts use the existing marker convention (no raw HTML), matching how memory excerpts are highlighted.
When no provider/API key is configured OR semantic search is disabled, search returns an empty/disabled result without throwing.
Unit tests cover ranking order on a fixture, excerpt extraction + markers, and the disabled/empty-state path.

items.title: Palette endpoint + Conversations group in the Ctrl+K palette

narrative.Action: Extend GET /api/palette/search?q=&limit= (route src/dashboard/server/routes/palette.ts) so its response gains a `conversations` array alongside the existing { memory, observations, summaries }, populated by the search item. Add a Conversations result group to src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandPalette.tsx, REUSING the existing excerpt-segment renderer for the highlight markers. Preserve the Phase-1 debounce (~250ms) and keyboard navigation; render conversation title + highlighted excerpt; handle loading / empty / no-results states. No regression to the existing palette groups.

subItems.title: GET /api/palette/search returns a `conversations` array without breaking the existing memory/observations/summaries fields.
CommandPalette renders a Conversations group showing conversation title + highlighted excerpt via the existing segment renderer.
Debounced query and keyboard navigation work for the new group; loading / empty / no-results states render correctly.
No regression in the Phase-1 palette groups (commands / memory / observations / issues).
A component test covers the Conversations group rendering and keyboard selection.

items.title: Open conversation deep-linked + scrolled to the matched message

narrative.Action: Selecting a conversation result navigates to /conv/:id and scrolls to the matched message using the sequence / byteOffset emitted by the parser, honored by ConversationPanel / MessagesTimeline. Thread the scroll target through the route/drawer so it also works on a cold deep-link load, not just when the conversation is already open.

subItems.title: Selecting a conversation result opens that conversation (drawer/route /conv/:id).
The conversation view scrolls to the matched message using sequence/byteOffset.
Deep-linking works from a cold load (conversation not already open), not only when already mounted.

items.title: Conversation Search settings section + bulk-index cost guard

narrative.Action: Add a Conversation Search settings section exposing embedding provider / model / API key, an enable-disable toggle, and a last-indexed indicator, REUSING the existing config keys (conversations.embeddings, embeddingProvider, embeddingModel, apiKeys) in src/lib/config-yaml.ts / src/lib/config.ts. Before a lazy bulk index whose estimated embedding cost would exceed ~$1, show the estimate and require explicit confirmation. Disabling semantic search must be fully supported and must make the palette Conversations path return the disabled/empty state with no embedding calls.

subItems.title: Settings expose provider / model / API key, an enable toggle, and a last-indexed indicator for Conversation Search.
An estimated token/$ cost is shown before a bulk (lazy) index; an index estimated above ~$1 requires explicit confirmation.
Disabling semantic search makes the palette Conversations path return the disabled/empty state with no embedding calls.
Settings persist via the existing config (conversations.embeddings / embeddingProvider / embeddingModel / apiKeys).
